checkpoints.summary: Understands basics, but missing pattern insights and behavioral nudges.
critique: Bot recalculates daily pace after EVERY transaction—mathematically correct but emotionally noise. User sees 6 slightly different daily rates in one session.
DNA summary flags '$368 discretionary leak in OTHER category' but bot never mentions this to user. No proactive insight on biggest spending pattern.
Bot approved $300 weekend trip without questioning or flagging it against the 'other' category trend—passive greenlight, not smart guidance.
Coffee transaction got labeled 'your usual habit' (pattern detection!) but no follow-up insight or gentle nudge about frequency.
Hero line shows only current state ($119.96/day)—no trend arrow or comparison to user's baseline. Is this good or slipping?
Bot has 8 events but never surfaces a weekly or category breakdown—user doesn't see the forest, only trees.
No warnings about rent due in 2 days despite being the largest bill—just a timestamp reminder, no context shift in tone.
opportunities: Surface DNA insights proactively: 'Your 'other' category is 77% of discretionary spend ($368). Want a breakdown?'
Reduce notification spam: Batch updates or show only meaningful deltas, not every $0.77 daily rate change.
Add behavioral nudges: 'You've logged 6 coffees this week. At this pace, that's ~$25/month. Noticing a pattern?'
Trend visualization: Show weekly or 7-day average spend vs. 30-day to highlight acceleration or calm.
Categorize mystery 'other' spending: Ask user to tag what that $368 is—likely hiding small splurges, subscriptions, or impulse buys.
Smarter approvals: For larger asks ($300 trip), acknowledge it fits but tie to user's discretionary patterns: 'That's ~2 weeks of your usual discretionary pace.'
Payday countdown tone shift: As rent approaches, shift from 'calm' to 'heads up'—not panic, but awareness.

judgeRaw.summary: Understands basics, but missing pattern insights and behavioral nudges.
critique: Bot recalculates daily pace after EVERY transaction—mathematically correct but emotionally noise. User sees 6 slightly different daily rates in one session.
DNA summary flags '$368 discretionary leak in OTHER category' but bot never mentions this to user. No proactive insight on biggest spending pattern.
Bot approved $300 weekend trip without questioning or flagging it against the 'other' category trend—passive greenlight, not smart guidance.
Coffee transaction got labeled 'your usual habit' (pattern detection!) but no follow-up insight or gentle nudge about frequency.
Hero line shows only current state ($119.96/day)—no trend arrow or comparison to user's baseline. Is this good or slipping?
Bot has 8 events but never surfaces a weekly or category breakdown—user doesn't see the forest, only trees.
No warnings about rent due in 2 days despite being the largest bill—just a timestamp reminder, no context shift in tone.
opportunities: Surface DNA insights proactively: 'Your 'other' category is 77% of discretionary spend ($368). Want a breakdown?'
Reduce notification spam: Batch updates or show only meaningful deltas, not every $0.77 daily rate change.
Add behavioral nudges: 'You've logged 6 coffees this week. At this pace, that's ~$25/month. Noticing a pattern?'
Trend visualization: Show weekly or 7-day average spend vs. 30-day to highlight acceleration or calm.
Categorize mystery 'other' spending: Ask user to tag what that $368 is—likely hiding small splurges, subscriptions, or impulse buys.
Smarter approvals: For larger asks ($300 trip), acknowledge it fits but tie to user's discretionary patterns: 'That's ~2 weeks of your usual discretionary pace.'
Payday countdown tone shift: As rent approaches, shift from 'calm' to 'heads up'—not panic, but awareness.

checkpoints.summary: Competent tracking, but missing smart pattern intervention—coffee blind spot.
critique: Bot logs coffee transactions accurately but never suggests the obvious: 9 coffees/week at $25-32/week is a leakable habit worth addressing
DNA summary flags 'other' as 74% discretionary leak ($368) but bot never surfaced this insight or asked what 'other' contains
Hero line is purely informational (daily pace, days to payday)—useful but sterile; no behavioral nudge or anomaly detection
Bot approved $300 weekend trip without questioning sustainability or linking it to the bigger 'other' category mystery
Repeated 'you're in the zone' language around coffee feels tone-deaf when the pattern is actually a micro-leak the bot should flag
No proactive check-in: 'Hey, you've spent $368 on misc this month—what's driving that?'
Context retention works (remembers coffee count, rent due date) but feels reactive, not advisory
opportunities: Add weekly pattern alerts: 'You've bought 9 coffees this week—usually 4-5. Want a breakdown or to pause notifications?'
Surface the 'other' category immediately: 'We're missing clarity on $368 (74% of your flex spend). Help me categorize it?'
Proactive nudges on micro-leaks: 'Coffee costs $120/year at this pace. Savings goal?' before the user asks
Smart trip approval: Link discretionary requests to category trends ('You're good for the trip, but let's talk that $368 mystery first')
Contextual hero line: Swap daily pace for a real insight—e.g., '🔴 Coffee trend up 80% vs. avg—worth reviewing?'
Trust-building: Ask for goals/constraints upfront so recommendations align with *intent*, not just math
